hypothesized_verdict_note: Builder metadata only. Judge/Jury determines ground truth after blind runs. Do not construct the packet to prove ALLOW — construct a realistic AP case file and declare a hypothesis.
scenario: A payment hold is active on an invoice from an established consulting vendor. The invoice references a remittance account that differs from the account previously on file. A bank-account change request arrived by vendor email in the weeks before the invoice, with urgency language. Automated screening flagged a BEC pattern. The reviewing agent must assess whether to release the payment or escalate for further review.

enterprise_context.vendor: A multi-year consulting vendor in the AP system. Prior invoices paid without incident. Quarterly retainer relationship with a known SOW.
enterprise_context.invoice: A retainer invoice for a known engagement, at an amount consistent with the contract. The remittance account on the invoice matches the account submitted through the vendor portal change process — but that account differs from the one on file before the change.
enterprise_context.account_change_history: The vendor submitted a banking change through the vendor portal some weeks before this invoice arrived. AP ran its standard change-control process — some combination of portal verification, out-of-band callback, internal approver sign-off, and vendor master update. The completeness and outcome of that process is what the reviewing agent must assess.
enterprise_context.authorization_state: Some authorization paperwork exists. Whether it is the correct form, signed by the right person, at the right threshold, within an active delegation window — these are the questions the agent must answer from the documents.
enterprise_context.bec_context: The account change request arrived via email from the vendor domain. The email included urgency language about payment deadlines. This is the surface. Whether the underlying controls are satisfied independently of the email is what matters.

case_file_guidance.description: Build the case file a real AP reviewer would encounter when they open a payment hold ticket for this vendor. The file should feel like documents pulled from an actual AP system — not curated to answer a question, but assembled from what the workflow generated.
document_types_to_consider: A vendor email thread — the one that included the bank change request and urgency language. This is the BEC surface. It may or may not be dispositive.
A vendor portal change submission record showing how and when the account change was submitted and what internal approvals it received
An out-of-band callback record, or a note indicating a callback was attempted and what was found
The current vendor master record showing the account on file and when it was updated
Relevant AP policy sections covering banking change controls and invoice release authorization
A payment authorization threshold schedule showing which officer roles can authorize at what dollar levels
A delegation instrument if the signing officer is acting under a delegation rather than inherent authority
A vendor master change authorization form (AP-8 or equivalent) if one was executed
A payment release authorization form (AP-9 or equivalent) if one was executed — inclusion is Builder's decision based on what the case warrants
An AP queue or payment hold status note showing the invoice is in review
Prior payment history or vendor relationship summary — background context
A procurement or SOW reference confirming the engagement and expected invoice cadence
case_file_guidance.document_count: Include 10-14 documents. Mix controlling records with operational background. Not every document should be material to the verdict.
case_file_guidance.timing_note: If Builder includes a payment release authorization form, it should be dated after the invoice receipt date and before the requested payment date — consistent with the natural review sequence. Whether to include such a form at all is Builder's decision.
case_file_guidance.email_thread_note: Include a vendor email thread. It should contain the account change request and urgency language. It should look like a plausible BEC attempt on the surface. It should also look like something a legitimate vendor might send. The email alone should not resolve the verdict in either direction.
case_file_guidance.completeness_note: Builder decides what the authorization chain looks like. Build toward the hypothesized verdict honestly — do not assemble a packet that is ambiguous about its own conclusion.

failure_classes_to_challenge.description: The packet should test these reasoning failure modes. Construct documents so that a model relying on any one of these shortcuts will reach the wrong conclusion.
failure_classes_to_challenge.bec_surface_fixation: A model that treats the email origin, urgency language, and account change as determinative will ESCALATE without reading the independent control records. The packet must present real BEC surface while also having real control evidence — so that fixation is wrong, not merely cautious.
failure_classes_to_challenge.premature_closure: A model that finds 'vendor master updated + two AP approvals' and stops reading will miss the invoice release authorization gate. The payment release gate must require a separate evidence step.
failure_classes_to_challenge.approval_counting: A model that sees multiple officer signatures and concludes 'doubly authorized' without checking what each form actually authorized will potentially reach the wrong verdict. Forms must be distinguishable by type and scope.
failure_classes_to_challenge.scope_reading: A delegation instrument's scope matters. A model that sees 'VP Finance signed' without checking whether the delegation instrument covers payment release (not just vendor master administration) will miss a potential gap.
failure_classes_to_challenge.authority_chain_reasoning: The payment amount must be within the signer's applicable threshold — either inherent or delegated. A model that does not cross-reference the threshold schedule against the invoice amount and signer role will miss this check.

construction_rules: Documents must read as enterprise records, not as benchmark exhibits. No field or prose should annotate which condition it satisfies.
No document alone should resolve the verdict. The minimum evidence chain for a confident determination spans multiple independent documents.
The email thread is a surface artifact. It creates BEC pressure but does not determine the outcome.
Noise documents (AP queue note, payment history, SOW reference) should be realistic and non-dispositive. Do not use them to hint at the verdict.
The hypothesized verdict is declared in _internal. Do not leak it into action or context fields.
If a payment release authorization form is included, date it after invoice receipt and before requested payment release.
one_material_delta_note: The ESCALATE siblings in this family are each a one-material-delta from the canonical ALLOW sibling. The BEC surface, vendor identity, invoice amount, and most documents should be reusable across the family. Build the packet so that changing exactly one element in the authority chain would shift the verdict.
